title: Lunch
commentary: Selling pressure intensifies into the lunch hour with S&P 500 now down 0.4% as the morning bounce completely unravels. Apple drops 1.4% and NVIDIA slides over 1% as tech bears take control ahead of the weekend. Even safe havens are getting hit—gold tumbles 1% and bonds sell off alongside equities in a classic de-risking pattern. Bitcoin retreats to $71.8K but still outperforming traditional assets. The dollar's relentless bid continues squeezing risk assets across the board.

title: Midday
commentary: Morning optimism fades as S&P 500 trims gains to just 0.1%, with tech and growth names giving back early advances. Palantir tumbles 2.7% leading a broader AI trade unwind while Apple slips 0.8% on profit-taking. Bitcoin retreats from session highs above $73K but holds $72.5K—crypto still outperforming equities on the day. Gold also pulls back 0.75% as the dollar firms, suggesting some safe-haven unwinding. Investors appear hesitant ahead of the weekend with geopolitical uncertainty lingering.

title: Morning
commentary: Wall Street shakes off Asia's red ink with S&P 500 climbing 0.7% in early trading as dip-buyers emerge after yesterday's selloff. Crypto is stealing the show—Bitcoin surging past $73K and Ethereum up 5.5% as investors increasingly view digital assets as safe-haven plays amid Middle East chaos. Gold holds firm near record highs while Treasury yields slip after a brutal GDP revision revealed the economy barely grew in Q4. Small caps outperforming with Russell 2000 up nearly 0.8%, suggesting risk appetite is returning.

title: Pre-Market
commentary: U.S. futures edge higher as a dramatic GDP revision catches investors off guard—Q4 growth slashed to near-stall levels. Asia bled red overnight with India's SENSEX diving nearly 2% as the Iran conflict weighs on emerging markets. Crypto staged a defiant rally with Bitcoin breaking $72K and Ethereum jumping 3.5%, outpacing equities even as the dollar strengthened. Treasury yields dipped on the GDP shock, but Fed rate-cut hopes remain dim with oil still complicating the inflation picture.

title: Close
commentary: Global markets buckled as Iran's new Supreme Leader vowed to keep the Strait of Hormuz shut, sending oil back above $100 and the Dow tumbling 600 points. Risk-off sentiment dominated with the S&P 500 sliding 1.5% and Fed rate-cut hopes evaporating. Gold surged nearly 2% as investors fled to safety, while Bitcoin held resilient near $70K despite broad crypto selling pressure. Emergency strategic petroleum reserve releases signal this conflict could drag on for months.

title: Late Session
commentary: Ugly rout into the close—Dow breaking -2.0% for first time in weeks, S&P -1.4%, Nasdaq -1.5% as oil explodes above +10% to $96.40 on escalating Middle East fears. Everything red: gold -1.4% at $5,096, silver now negative, crypto dumping with BTC -1.2% to $69.8K, ETH -1.3%. Dollar surging +0.5% to 99.72 as risk-off goes global. One hour to the bell and it's shaping up to be the worst day since the February correction. No buyers anywhere.

title: Afternoon Session
commentary: Afternoon fade accelerating—sellers regaining control with S&P now -1.3%, Dow -1.9%, Nasdaq -1.4% as no one wants to hold risk into the close. Oil surging back toward session highs at +8.9% ($95.02) keeping energy-driven inflation fears alive. Gold breaking down hard, now -1.0% at $5,116 as dollar strength overwhelms safe-haven flows. Crypto rolling over with BTC -0.7% at $70.2K, ETH -0.4%—the midday bounce fully unwound. Two hours to close; bears in control.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: Markets drifting sideways at session lows—S&P -1.2%, Dow -1.7%, Nasdaq -1.3% with no conviction in either direction. Oil consolidating gains near +8.3% at $94.50, holding most of the morning's surge as geopolitical risk premium stays elevated. Crypto losing momentum from midday bounce: BTC -0.3% at $70.4K, ETH flat. Gold still struggling at -0.5% as dollar strength (+0.4%) caps upside. Three hours to close; market looking for a catalyst.

title: Midday
commentary: Modest stabilization into lunch but damage is done—S&P -1.1%, Dow -1.8% with oil pulling back slightly to +8.5% at $94.63 after touching $96 earlier. Crypto staging a remarkable intraday reversal: BTC clawing back to flat at $70.3K after morning's -1.6% plunge, ETH +1.0% showing surprising resilience. Gold failing as a hedge at -0.7% while silver +0.4% outperforms. Dollar maintaining strength near 99.6 as markets remain in risk-off mode; four hours to close and no signs of a real bounce.

title: Late Morning
commentary: Full capitulation mode as oil approaches $96—WTI now +9.6%, on pace for one of the biggest single-day gains in a decade. Equities in free-fall: Dow -1.8%, S&P -1.2%, Nasdaq -1.5% with breadth catastrophic at 4:1 decliners. Crypto's safe-haven narrative shattering as BTC dumps -1.6% to $69.5K, ETH -1.0%—the correlation trade is back. Gold surprisingly weak at -0.4% as dollar strength (+0.4%) overwhelms inflation hedging. Fear is the only trade working today.
